reason: Review against project boundaries, manual acceptance expectations, and forbidden scope expansions.
Check product acceptance for devices, input meter, output fallback, tray persistence, and excluded features.
Check Wails/WebView2/LiveKit ownership boundaries and no Go audio pipeline.
Check terminology and product meaning of system tray, microphone device, output device, and local settings.
Check UI labels, accessibility expectations, and no member-volume/output-test-tone leakage.
Check no Electron fallback and Wails shell behavior remains scoped.
Check audio remains in WebView2/LiveKit and output unsupported fallback is documented.
Check deterministic tests and HITL limitations are reported honestly.
Check frontend/Wails scaffold validation commands and build contracts.
Check layer-boundary contracts and cleanup/error handling.
Check media logic is centralized rather than duplicated in components.
